title: Considering the ambiguous case
text: When using the Law of Sines to find an angle, there can be two possible solutions if the side opposite the angle is shorter than the adjacent side, but longer than the height.

title: Identify the acute angle
text: What was the acute angle $$A_1$$ found in the previous step?

title: Finding the second possible angle
text: If $$A_1$$ is the acute angle found, the second possible angle $$A_2$$ is $$180**{\circ} $$-$$ $$A_1$$.

title: Calculate the second angle
text: Calculate $$180**{\circ} $$-$$ $$A_1$$ to find the second possible value for $$A$$. $$Round$$ $$to$$ $$the$$ $$nearest$$ $$tenth$$.

title: Checking validity
text: Ensure that the sum of the angles in the triangle ($$A_2 + B$$) is less than $$180**{\circ}$$.
